column1: Freyja — Anthropometric Reference: Body Segment Inertial Parameters

column2: Single source of truth for segment mass, centre-of-mass, and full inertia tensor. Every CAD/control decision references these values. Normalized parameters live here once; the 170 cm / 65 kg instantiation is computed by formula so a stature or mass change propagates from one cell.
65 kg  (set on 'BSIP Reference' B1 — blue input)
1700 mm  (set on 'BSIP Reference' B2 — blue input)
Dumas (2007) female = adjusted from Young et al. (1983), USAF women. Young, fit cohort — NOT a population-median female. Document deviations against this, do not assume it is a median.
+x anterior, +y cranial (up), +z to the subject's right.

Origin = right hip joint centre. x = anterior, normal to the plane of the two ASIS and the PSIS midpoint. z = parallel to the inter-ASIS line, pointing right. y = up, completing the triad. Build the Fusion pelvis CSYS to match this so the CoM vector and tensor drop in with no re-rotation.
has its own SCS per Dumas/ISB. CoM vectors and tensors are expressed in that segment's frame — match each Fusion segment CSYS to its Dumas frame.
Anatomical neutral standing. Joint angle sign conventions per ISB JCS (adopt Grood-Suntay for the knee when that row is added).
kg
mm (CAD-native)
kg·m²  (biomech/sim standard; ×1e6 → kg·mm² for Fusion if needed)

Enter EXACTLY as published: mass as fraction of body mass; CoM and radii of gyration as PERCENT of segment length (the sheet divides by 100). Entering them as published = fewest transcription transforms = fewest errors.
Dumas (2007) female — PRIMARY. + Corrigendum 2007b for Head & Neck. + Dumas (2015) for Thorax & Abdomen. de Leva female = cross-check (limbs only — see below).
Harrington (2007), pelvic-dimension regression — PRIMARY. Bell (1990) = secondary cross-check.
Landmark midpoints (femoral epicondyles / malleoli), not a regression.
Anatomical ceiling: Boone & Azen / AAOS. Functional target: gait/stair/sit-to-stand kinematics. Keep as two columns.
MakeHuman mesh = authoritative (envelope-first). ANSUR II female = reality-check on the mesh's driving girths/breadths.
Derived sanity read-out from Fusion mass properties, not an independent target. Dempster/Winter values for eyeballing only.

Sheet uses  I_ij = SIGN(r_ij)·m·(r_ij·L)²  with r_ij the signed off-diagonal radius of gyration (% of L). CONFIRM this against Dumas (2007) inertia-matrix equation before trusting the products — the sign is the single easiest term to get wrong.
Clean only for limb segments. de Leva carries one trunk segment; Dumas splits Thorax/Abdomen/Pelvis. For the trunk, compare LUMPED (sum Dumas's three) — not row-by-row.

Dumas 'pelvis' is the whole biomechanical segment: bone + gluteal/pelvic soft tissue + lower viscera. The target is Freyja's ENTIRE pelvis segment (structure + battery + flesh + skin), NOT the ~8 kg structural assembly alone. Do not compare 8 kg of hardware to the Dumas segment mass directly.

Dumas normalizes the pelvis by its segment length = hip-JC midpoint to the lumbosacral (L5/S1) joint centre. Pin this exact endpoint from the paper before instantiating — pelvis length varies most by definition between sources.
Fill the YELLOW cells on 'BSIP Reference': segment length L (from MakeHuman/your geometry) and the Dumas normalized values (transcribe from your copy of the paper).
Targets (mass kg, CoM mm, inertia kg·m²) compute automatically.
On 'Validation', paste Fusion mass-properties into the YELLOW achieved cells; deviations compute. Log any deliberate departure from biology with magnitude + expected control cost.
Check the count-weighted mass-fraction sum (≈1.000) reconstructs body mass — your transcription tripwire.
Blue = hardcoded input   •   Black = formula   •   Green = link to another sheet   •   Yellow fill = needs your entry
